perturbation.original_text: SPONSORSHIP AGREEMENT This agreement (the "Agreement") is made effective this 1st day of April, 2018 ("Effective Date") between Fruit of Life Productions LLC, ("Promoter") and Eco Science Solutions, Inc.,("Sponsor"), 1135 Makawao Avenue, Suite 103-188, Makawao, Hawaii, 96768.
perturbation.changed_text: SPONSORSHIP AGREEMENT This agreement (the "Agreement") is made effective this 1st day of April, 2018 ("Effective Date") between Fruit of Life Productions LLC, ("Promoter") and Eco Science Solutions, Inc.,("Participant"), 1135 Makawao Avenue, Suite 103-188, Makawao, Hawaii, 96768.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Sponsor' to 'Participant' in the agreement's introduction introduces uncertainty. Throughout the contract, rights and obligations are assigned to the 'Sponsor.' By redefining the Eco Science Solutions, Inc. as 'Participant' it creates a contradiction as to whether the company is still entitled to the sponsorship benefits detailed in the agreement, specifically in the section outlining 'Sponsorship Benefits for Presenting Partner Sponsor'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Contribution by Sponsor: In consideration for the right to sponsor the Kaya Fest and to be acknowledged by Fruit of Life Productions LLC., as a Promoter of the event during the term of this Agreement, Sponsor agrees to contract with Fruit of Life Productions LLC., for the amount of $250,000.00 to be paid in full upon signing of this agreement. Bank Wire Transformation Information See Attached Wire Instructions Sponsorship Benefits for Presenting Partner Sponsor: * Main Stage named after your brand * 4 10x10 on site vendor booths * 50 VIP Sponsor Passes / 50 GA tickets for both days * 4 Parking passes * Opportunity to participate in after party * Banner placement in venue (10) * Approved audio/video assets to be provided as promotional use for Herbo * Name and phrase called out on stage between performers set * Your logo and a link from our website to your website * Your logo on video wall * Your company name and logo as a presenting sponsor * Banner at main entrance of venue * On stage banner placement * Logo in Backstage/VIP area * Mention on social media * Logo on Step and Repeat * Logo on all promotional print
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Sponsor' to 'Participant' directly contradicts the contradicted text because the contradicted text outlines benefits and obligations specifically for the 'Sponsor'. By redefining Eco Science Solutions, Inc. as 'Participant' in the agreement's introduction, it creates a significant ambiguity and contradiction as to whether they are still entitled to the benefits listed under 'Sponsorship Benefits for Presenting Partner Sponsor' and obligated by the 'Contribution by Sponsor' clause, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes.

perturbation.original_text: Sponsors are bound by the same terms and conditions, if exhibiting, as all other vendors of event. Sponsors must have their own liability insurance with limits of one million dollars.
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsors are bound by the same terms and conditions, if exhibiting, as all other vendors of event. Exhibitors must have their own liability insurance with limits of one million dollars.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Sponsors' to 'Exhibitors' in the section on Exhibiting introduces ambiguity. While sponsors who exhibit are bound by vendor terms, it creates uncertainty if all 'Sponsors' need million-dollar liability insurance regardless of whether they are exhibiting. This is because it is changing who needs to hold the liability insurance, since the original section 'Sponsors' included also Sponsors who didn't need to exhibit, this leads to the change and in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sponsors are bound by the same terms and conditions, if exhibiting, as all other vendors of event. Exhibitors must have their own liability insurance with limits of one million dollars.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Sponsors' with 'Exhibitors' regarding liability insurance. The original text states that sponsors who exhibit are bound by vendor terms. The change introduces ambiguity, as it becomes uncertain whether all sponsors, regardless of exhibiting, need the insurance. While the explanation suggests this creates uncertainty, the text in the 'Contradicted Text' section is exactly the same as the 'Changed Text' section, and doesn't create a clear contradiction, since both sections read the same. The original text and changed text are only slightly different.

perturbation.original_text: The Sponsor understands that they are free to seek legal advice on the content of this agreement and applicable law from independent counsel.
perturbation.changed_text: The Investor understands that they are free to seek legal advice on the content of this agreement and applicable law from independent counsel.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Sponsor' to 'Investor' shifts the understanding and responsibility of seeking legal advice. This introduces ambiguity, especially regarding the agreement's enforceability against Eco Science Solutions, Inc. Since the agreement defines Eco Science Solutions, Inc. as the 'Sponsor' and not as the 'Investor', there is an in-text contradiction of if the Sponsor actually has to seek legal advice.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sponsor: Eco Science Solutions, Inc. By: /s/Jeffery Taylor Name: Jeffery Taylor Title: CEO Date: 4/01/2018 Promoter: Fruit of Life Productions LLC: By:/s/Stella McLaughlan Name: Stella McLaughlan Title: Event Coordinator Date: 4/01/2018 3 Source: ECO SCIENCE SOLUTIONS, INC., 8-K, 4/6/2018
perturbation.justification: The original text states the 'Sponsor' is free to seek legal advice. The changed text replaces 'Sponsor' with 'Investor'. The contradicted text defines 'Sponsor' as 'Eco Science Solutions, Inc.'. By changing 'Sponsor' to 'Investor', the agreement now implies that the Investor, rather than the specifically defined Sponsor (Eco Science Solutions, Inc.), is advised to seek legal counsel, creating a contradiction because the Sponsor and Investor are two separate entities as defined by the agreement. This constitutes a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.
